description: Narrative adventure song that tells a fairy tale story with magical elements and a happy ending
suno_tags: whimsical folk pop, warm female vocals, acoustic guitar, flute, strings, storytelling, magical, 95 BPM
vocal_style: Warm expressive adult female voice, storyteller quality, shifts between narrative and singing, gentle and enchanting

tips: Structure as a mini story: setup (V1), journey (V2), challenge (Bridge), resolution (V3)
Use 'once upon a time' or fairy tale language to set the tone
Include a clear hero, a quest, and a happy resolution
The chorus should be the story's emotional theme, not plot
Bridge is the dramatic moment — the challenge or turning point

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]

[Verse 1]
Once upon a time so long ago
A little bear set out through the snow
With a scarf of red and boots of blue
Searching for a friend, brave and true

[Chorus]
Every story needs a heart that's brave
Every journey finds the ones we save
Through the woods and over the stream
Every fairy tale begins with a dream

[Verse 2]
Through the forest dark, past the wishing well
Past the singing frogs and the magic bell
Little bear kept walking, step by step
Following a path where the fireflies slept

[Chorus]
Every story needs a heart that's brave
Every journey finds the ones we save
Through the woods and over the stream
Every fairy tale begins with a dream

[Bridge]
Then the wind blew cold and the path was gone
Little bear felt lost, all alone at dawn
But a tiny light said, "Follow me!"
A firefly friend, shining wild and free

[Verse 3]
Side by side they walked to the mountain top
Hand in hand, they knew they'd never stop
And when the sun rose golden in the sky
Little bear smiled and said, "That's why"

[Chorus]
Every story needs a heart that's brave
Every journey finds the ones we save
Through the woods and over the stream
Every fairy tale begins with a dream

[Outro]
[Soft]
And they lived happily... ever after.
[End]
do: Tell a complete story with beginning, middle, and end
Use a clear hero character that kids can root for
Make the bridge the dramatic turning point of the story
End with resolution and a happy, satisfying conclusion
dont: Never use children's choir or kids singing in suno_tags
Avoid truly scary villains or threatening situations
Don't leave the story unresolved — kids need closure
Avoid overly complex plots with multiple subplots
Don't use archaic fairy tale language kids won't understand
